behavior_summary: U8P_EBROCK::look is the object's U8P_EBROCK::look/examine handler. When the player inspects the object it reads the item's frame index (via Item::getFrame(this)) and displays a short descriptive bark corresponding to that frame.
Frame values 0–4 map to five distinct barks: 'glowing obelisk tip', 'glowing Heart of Earth', 'glowing Tear of the Seas', 'glowing Breath of Wind', and 'glowing Tongue of Flame'.
After emitting the appropriate bark the process suspends to allow the message to be presented to the player.
U8P_EBROCK::use exists as a process stub/utility with no observable behavior in the provided excerpt.
notable_control_flow: U8P_EBROCK::look reads an item frame/index into a local (local1) and uses that value to branch to one of five message outputs.
Each branch issues a single short Bark and then performs suspend (ends/yields the U8P_EBROCK::look process).
No global flags are read or written by either function in the decompiled excerpt.
U8P_EBROCK::use contains no calls or side effects in the excerpt and does not affect control flow elsewhere.
open_questions: What happens for frame values outside 0–4 (is there a fallback/default bark or no output)?
Is U8P_EBROCK::use intended to be implemented elsewhere or by game data (it is a stub in this excerpt)?
Are the bark strings shared resources referenced elsewhere or unique to this object instance?
Is local1 the canonical name in the original source or a decompiler temporary (the role is clear but the original identifier is unknown)?

main_function.name: U8P_EBROCK::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short descriptive message when the player examines this item, selecting text based on the item's current frame/index.
entry_conditions: Invoked by a player U8P_EBROCK::look/examine action (process type 0x0208).
Runs when the engine requests the object's U8P_EBROCK::look handler; it reads the instance frame to choose output.

function_map.name: U8P_EBROCK::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that maps item frame values to short descriptive barks.

function_map.name: U8P_EBROCK::use
function_map.role: Utility/U8P_EBROCK::use process present as a stub in the excerpt; no observable behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's current frame/index (uword) to determine which descriptive bark to show.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_EBROCK::look is the object's U8P_EBROCK::look/examine handler. When the player inspects the object it reads the item's frame index (via Item::getFrame(this)) and displays a short descriptive bark corresponding to that frame.
